{{Quote|By the darkness that I am, the time I have longed for has finally come. The end of the Jedi Knights...I can taste it! There will be no one left to oppose my will!|Palpatine|<ref name="Dark Empire II 6" />}}
A '''battle''' occurred on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[New Alderaan/Legends|New Alderaan]] in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]] between the reborn [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] forces and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. A party of [[Dark Side Elite]] under Palpatine's new [[Executor (Imperial rank)|Executor]] [[Xecr Nist]] infiltrated the settlement with the objective of assassinating the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and kidnapping the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Solo twins (disambiguation)|Solo twins]]. However, they were intercepted by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and most of the party was killed.

Meanwhile, the settlement came under attack from a complement of Imperial [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] walkers which quickly overwhelmed the lightly-armed defenders. However, the villagers were saved by the swift arrival of several [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]] including the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] which destroyed several walkers and managed to evacuate the villagers.

==Prelude==
Prior to the battle, the Emperor had sent a contingent of [[Scarab Mark VI assassin droid|Scarab Droids]] to murder [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] in his sleep. The Emperor also sent his [[Executor (Imperial rank)|Executor]] [[Xecr Nist]] and his second in command [[Tedryn-Sha]] to kidnap the [[Solo twins (disambiguation)|Solo twins]]. Unfortunately for them, [[Jem Ysanna]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] confronted them and killed them while [[Vima-Da-Boda]] destroyed the Scarab Droids.<ref name="Dark Empire II 6" />

==The battle==
[[File:Second_battle_of_new_alderaan.jpg|thumb|left|150px|[[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-ATs]] hit by ship-fire.]]

In retaliation for the [[Battle of the Emperor's Citadel|assault on his Citadel at Byss]], the Emperor sent a force of [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-ATs]] to destroy the Rebel settlement at [[New Alderaan/Legends|New Alderaan]]. The AT-ATs were the latest generation of the vehicle class and were equipped with the most powerful Imperial [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] and the new [[X-80 Power Cell]]s. As a result, many buildings and gun emplacements were destroyed. Just as the village was about to be massacred, the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], ''[[Starhook Ten]]'', ''[[Bespin Bandit]]'', ''[[Hyperspace Marauder]]'' and a squadron of X-wings came to the rescue. These new arrivals destroyed several of the AT-ATs. ''Starhook Ten'' landed and opened her cargo bay to the surviving refugees. However, the Rebels were forced to flee and New Alderaan was lost to the Empire.<ref name="Dark Empire II 6" />

==Behind the scenes==
The second battle at New Alderaan first appeared in the issue [[Dark Empire II 6|''Dark Empire II'' 6]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]'' series of comics. The battle was conceived and written by authors [[Tom Veitch]], illustrated and colored by colorist [[Cam Kennedy]]. Veith used this battle of the climax of the plot of ''Dark Empire II''.

The battle would eventually be featured in the [[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'' audio drama]], which witnessed the skirmish take on a whole new personality through the voice acting of [[John Cygan]] (Skywalker), Joe Hacker (Han and Brand), Jim Ward (Kam Solusar) and [[Glynnis Talken Campbell|Glynnis Talken]] (Salla Zend, Vima and Jem).

However, one major difference between the comic and audio drama is the depiction of Dark-sider Xerc Nist's part in the mission to New Alderaan. In contradiction to ''Dark Empire II'', Nist does not participate in the fight between the Dark Jedi, Solusar, Rayf Ysanna, and Brand in the audio adaptation. Instead, he takes the Solo children and guards them as his darksiders fight the Jedi.

After all members of the Dark Side Elite are killed, Nist tries to escape with the twins and almost reaches his ship, but is stopped dead by two blaster bolts fired by Leia Organa Solo. Nevertheless, Nist appears in the [[Empire's End (audio drama)|audio adaptation]] of ''[[Star Wars: Empire's End]]'' without any explanation on how he survived.
